deep_dive.title: Social learning community detection with nonlinear interaction
deep_dive.abstract: Conventional community detection requires centralized network data, making it unsuitable for distributed or privacy-preserving systems. In this paper, we demonstrate that macroscopic graph partitioning can emerge purely from strictly local, privacy preserving interactions driven by social learning. By reframing clustering as a symmetry-breaking process within nonlinear opinion dynamics, we show that exchanging saturated state dependent signal (like public actions) forces a network to naturally fracture along its sparsest cuts. We mathematically establish the spectral conditions under which dense core communities lock into stable, polarized states, robustly resisting external influence. To apply this mechanism, we propose three decentralized algorithms, leading up to the Score-based Edge Reliability (SER) framework. By evaluating network ties across multiple independent discussion topics, SER statistically bypasses the errors of traditional greedy bisections and naturally isolates structurally ambiguous frontier nodes. Validations on the ABCD benchmark and the real-world Ngogo chimpanzee network confirm that our fully decentralized approach matches the accuracy of globally optimized heuristics (e.g., Louvain, Leiden) up to a theoretical limit of detectable graphs.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: graph partitioning emerges from social learning
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 图分区通过社会学习涌现
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 图分区通过社会学习涌现
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 图分区通过社会学习涌现
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Demonstrates that macroscopic graph partitioning can emerge from strictly local privacy-preserving interactions via social learning, proposing decentralized algorithms.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对传统社区检测需要集中式数据的问题，证明仅通过局部隐私保护的社会学习即可涌现宏观图分区。将聚类重构为非线性意见动态中的对称破缺过程，交换饱和状态信号迫使网络沿最稀疏切割自然分裂。建立了谱条件，并提出三种去中心化算法，实现了分布式社区发现。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对传统社区检测需要集中式数据的问题，证明仅通过局部隐私保护的社会学习即可涌现宏观图分区。将聚类重构为非线性意见动态中的对称破缺过程，交换饱和状态信号迫使网络沿最稀疏切割自然分裂。建立了谱条件，并提出三种去中心化算法，实现了分布式社区发现。

deep_dive.title: Policy Gradient for Continuous-Time Robust Markov Decision Processes
deep_dive.abstract: The framework of robust Markov decision processes (RMDPs) allows the design of reinforcement learning agents that satisfy performance guarantees under worst-case transition dynamics. Traditional RMDPs consider discrete-time dynamics and recently, sample-efficient policy gradient algorithms have been considered in this context. This paper investigates policy gradient algorithms within a continuous-time RMDP framework. Policy gradients and adversarial gradients are derived using pathwise and adjoint-based formulas for stochastic and ordinary differential equations. We propose double-loop optimisers to obtain linear convergence in the oracle-based setting and an $\tilde{\mathcal{O}}(\frac{1}{ε^2})$ sample complexity in the sample-based setting in an analysis which also derives novel tools for the framework of undiscounted total cost MDPs. Additionally, we propose mean-field optimisers as distributional optimisers with an $\tilde{\mathcal{O}}(\frac{1}{K})$ oracle-based convergence rate and an $\tilde{\mathcal{O}}(\frac{N^2}ε)$ sample complexity under $N$-particle approximation. The effectiveness of continuous-time policy gradient algorithms is confirmed for both optimisers on continuous-time RMDPs with neural ordinary differential equation dynamics.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: develops policy gradient algorithms for continuous-time robust MDPs
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 开发了连续时间鲁棒MDP的策略梯度算法
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 开发了连续时间鲁棒MDP的策略梯度算法
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 开发了连续时间鲁棒MDP的策略梯度算法
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper extends policy gradient RL to continuous-time robust Markov decision processes, deriving gradients via pathwise and adjoint methods and achieving linear convergence in oracle setting.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 鲁棒MDP通常考虑离散时间，本文将其扩展到连续时间框架。利用随机微分方程的路径和伴随公式推导出策略梯度和对抗梯度，并提出双层优化器，在基于oracle的设置下达到线性收敛。这项工作为连续时间鲁棒RL提供了理论保证和实用算法。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 鲁棒MDP通常考虑离散时间，本文将其扩展到连续时间框架。利用随机微分方程的路径和伴随公式推导出策略梯度和对抗梯度，并提出双层优化器，在基于oracle的设置下达到线性收敛。这项工作为连续时间鲁棒RL提供了理论保证和实用算法。

quick_skim.title: Representation Learning Enables Scalable Multitask Deep Reinforcement Learning
quick_skim.abstract: Scaling reinforcement learning (RL) to diverse multitask settings remains a central challenge. While recent advances in model-based RL achieve strong performance, they rely on planning and complex training pipelines, making it unclear which components are essential for scalability. We revisit this question and argue that the primary driver of scalable multitask RL is not model-based control, but \emph{representation learning}. In particular, we show that combining predictive, model-based representations with high-capacity value function approximation is sufficient to achieve strong performance, even without planning. We evaluate a simple model-free algorithm, MR.Q, coupled with auxiliary predictive objectives into a scalable actor-critic architecture. This approach outperforms a recent world-model-based method and a range of deep RL baselines across a diverse suite of multitask continuous control tasks, while significantly reducing computational overhead and improving wall-clock efficiency. We observe consistent improvements with increased model capacity and show through ablations that predictive representation learning is critical for performance.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: representation learning enables scalable multitask deep reinforcement learning
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 表示学习实现可扩展的多任务深度强化学习
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 表示学习实现可扩展的多任务深度强化学习
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 表示学习实现可扩展的多任务深度强化学习
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Argues that predictive representations and high-capacity value functions are sufficient for scalable multitask RL without planning.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 多任务强化学习扩展性挑战。本文论证表示学习是扩展性的关键，而非基于模型的规划。提出MR.Q算法，结合预测性表示和高容量值函数近似，在多种任务上表现优异，且无需复杂规划。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 多任务强化学习扩展性挑战。本文论证表示学习是扩展性的关键，而非基于模型的规划。提出MR.Q算法，结合预测性表示和高容量值函数近似，在多种任务上表现优异，且无需复杂规划。

quick_skim.title: Online KL-Regularized Reinforcement Learning with Function Approximation under Misspecification
quick_skim.abstract: We study KL-regularized contextual bandits and episodic reinforcement learning (RL) under general function approximation with model misspecification. Existing guarantees rely on realizability and therefore do not extend to misspecified models, where classical regret bounds may fail. This work introduces KL misspecification formulations for contextual bandits and episodic RL and analyzes regression-based algorithms with Gibbs policy updates. High-probability KL-regret guarantees with explicit misspecification terms are established, recovering the standard realizable KL-regularized setting as a special case.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: KL-regularized reinforcement learning with function approximation under misspecification
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 在模型错误设定下使用函数近似的KL正则化强化学习
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 在模型错误设定下使用函数近似的KL正则化强化学习
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 在模型错误设定下使用函数近似的KL正则化强化学习
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Studies KL-regularized RL under general function approximation with model misspecification, providing high-probability KL-regret bounds for contextal bandits and episodic RL.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对模型错误设定下强化学习理论不完善的问题，研究了在一般函数近似下带有KL正则化的上下文赌博机和情节强化学习。提出了KL错误设定公式，分析了基于回归的Gibbs策略更新算法，建立了显式错误项的高概率KL遗憾界，覆盖了标准可实现KL正则化设置。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对模型错误设定下强化学习理论不完善的问题，研究了在一般函数近似下带有KL正则化的上下文赌博机和情节强化学习。提出了KL错误设定公式，分析了基于回归的Gibbs策略更新算法，建立了显式错误项的高概率KL遗憾界，覆盖了标准可实现KL正则化设置。
